zh.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy於大約1303年出生在英國德比夏爾特盧斯利。她於1321年與John Fraunceys在英國德比夏爾德比結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1364年在英國德比夏爾梅爾布恩享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy于大约1303年出生在英国德比夏爾特盧斯利。她于1321年与John Fraunceys在英国德比夏爾德比结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1364年在英国德比夏爾梅爾布恩享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beaufoy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name in the case of the Domesday Book tenants from Beaufour (Calvados), which is recorded as Belfou, Beaufou in 1100 and as Bellefai in about 1160 ( Tengvik ).

variants.name: Domesday Book
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn. In the Midlands and East Anglia this is based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn, anglicized as Beorn; in the south-west it is from Old English Beorn; both names are from a word meaning ‘warrior’. Some of the following surname bearers may alternatively belong with other senses below.

2 English: status name from Middle English barn (Old Scandinavian barn) ‘child’. This term is found in Domesday Book as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child ( see Child ).

3 English: locative name, nickname for residence near or employment at a barn, Middle English bern (Old English bere-ærn). See also Barnes . Some of the post-medieval bearers may alternatively belong with (1) or (2).

variants.name: Tengvik
meanings: Norman, English: locative name in the case of the Domesday Book tenants from Beaufour (Calvados), which is recorded as Belfou, Beaufou in 1100 and as Bellefai in about 1160 ( Tengvik ).

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy was born about 1303, in Trusley, Derbyshire, England. She married John Fraunceys in 1321, in Derby, Derbyshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1364, in Melbourne, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy est née environ 1303, à Trusley, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé John Fraunceys en 1321, à Derby, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1364, à Melbourne, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy wurde um 1303 in Trusley, Derbyshire, England geboren. Sie hat 1321 in Derby, Derbyshire, England John Fraunceys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1364 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Melbourne, Derbyshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy è nata il circa 1303 nel Trusley, Derbyshire, England. Lei sposato John Fraunceys nel 1321, nel Derby, Derbyshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1364, nel Melbourne, Derbyshire, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoyは約1303年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアトルスリーで生まれた。1321年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアデルビーでJohn Fraunceysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1364年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアメルボウルンで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy(은)는 대략 1303년 영국 더비실르 트루슬리에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1321년 영국 더비실르 더비에서 John Fraunceys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1364년 영국 더비실르 멜보우른에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy nasceu aproximadamente 1303, em Trusley, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com John Fraunceys em 1321, em Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1364, em Melbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy родилась около 1303 г. в местности трусли, дирбишир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Fraunceys в 1321 г. в местности дирби, дирбишир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1364 г. в местности мелбурн, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Matilda Margaret Beaufoy nació aproximadamente 1303, en Trusley, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con John Fraunceys en 1321, en Derby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1364, en Melbourne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
